title: New Zealand legalizes same-sex marriage
description: New Zealand's parliament passed a marriage equality law in 2013 and it took effect the same year, making the country the first in Oceania where same-sex couples could marry.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Uruguay legalizes same-sex marriage
description: Uruguay's marriage equality law passed and took effect in 2013, making it the second South American country to open marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: France legalizes same-sex marriage
description: France enacted a law in 2013 that opened both marriage and its associated rights to same-sex couples, with the change taking effect the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage becomes available across Brazil
description: A 2013 national judicial ruling required Brazilian registrars to perform same-sex marriages, making marriage available to same-sex couples throughout the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Six US states adopt same-sex marriage
description: During 2013 same-sex marriage was adopted and took effect in the US states of Delaware, Rhode Island, Minnesota, New Jersey, Hawaii and New Mexico, through a mix of legislation and court decisions.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Several Native American tribal nations adopt same-sex marriage
description: In 2013 same-sex marriage took effect in several sovereign tribal jurisdictions in the United States, including the Confederated Tribes of the Colville Reservation, the Little Traverse Bay Bands of Odawa Indians, the Pokagon Band of Potawatomi Indians, the Cheyenne and Arapaho Tribes, the Leech Lake Band of Ojibwe, the Grand Portage Band of Chippewa and the Iipay Nation of Santa Ysabel.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage resumes in California
description: Same-sex couples in California regained the ability to marry in 2013, after litigation over the state's Proposition 8 ban ended and marriages restarted.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: England and Wales pass same-sex marriage law
description: The UK parliament approved legislation in 2013 permitting same-sex couples to marry in England and Wales, with the first ceremonies to follow the next year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Illinois passes same-sex marriage law
description: The US state of Illinois passed a same-sex marriage bill in 2013, with the law scheduled to take effect the following year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Australian Capital Territory marriage law passed, then struck down
description: The Australian Capital Territory enacted a same-sex marriage law in 2013, but the measure was overturned shortly afterwards, ending the marriages performed under it.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Maryland
description: Maryland's voter-approved same-sex marriage law came into force in 2013, allowing couples in the US state to marry.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Supreme Court strikes down DOMA Section 3
description: The Supreme Court of the United States ruled in 2013 that Section 3 of the Defense of Marriage Act was unconstitutional, extending federal recognition to same-sex marriages performed under state law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Oregon recognizes out-of-state same-sex marriages
description: In 2013 the US state of Oregon began recognizing same-sex marriages performed in other jurisdictions, even though such marriages could not yet be performed in the state.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Campeche enacts civil unions
description: The Mexican state of Campeche passed a civil union law in 2013 and it came into force the same year, giving same-sex couples legal recognition.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Civil unions take effect in Colorado
description: Colorado's civil union law came into force in 2013, letting same-sex couples in the US state register their partnerships.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Costa Rica enacts limited partnership recognition
description: In 2013 Costa Rica adopted a legal change giving same-sex couples a limited form of partnership recognition, which took effect the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Zealand opens joint adoption to same-sex couples
description: Alongside its 2013 marriage reform, New Zealand legalized joint adoption by same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: France opens joint adoption to same-sex couples
description: France's 2013 marriage equality reform also legalized adoption by same-sex couples, giving married couples the same adoption rights regardless of gender.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: India's Supreme Court recriminalizes same-sex relations
description: In December 2013 the Supreme Court of India reinstated the colonial-era Section 377 of the penal code, reversing a 2009 lower-court ruling and making consensual same-sex relations a crime again.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cyprus bans discrimination on sexual orientation and gender identity
description: Cyprus adopted anti-discrimination legislation in 2013 covering both sexual orientation and gender identity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Puerto Rico bans discrimination on sexual orientation and gender identity
description: The US territory of Puerto Rico enacted legislation in 2013 outlawing discrimination based on sexual orientation and gender identity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Delaware adds gender identity to its anti-discrimination law
description: The US state of Delaware extended its anti-discrimination protections to cover gender identity in 2013.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Two Canadian provinces add gender identity and expression protections
description: In 2013 the Canadian provinces of Newfoundland and Labrador and Prince Edward Island added gender identity and gender expression to their human-rights protections.
region: Newfoundland and Labrador; Prince Edward Island

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Virginia takes executive action against anti-LGBTQ discrimination
description: The US state of Virginia adopted an executive measure in 2013 addressing discrimination against LGBTQ people in state employment.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ukraine holds its first pride march
description: Ukraine's first pride march took place in 2013 in Kyiv, despite strong opposition, marking the country's first public LGBTQ demonstration of this kind.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Montenegro holds its first pride march
description: Montenegro saw its first pride march in 2013, the country's first public LGBTQ rights demonstration.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Curaçao holds its first pride march
description: The Caribbean island of Curaçao held its first pride march in 2013.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kathleen Wynne becomes Canada's first openly LGBT premier
description: Kathleen Wynne won the Ontario Liberal leadership on 26 January 2013 and was sworn in as premier of Ontario on 11 February, becoming the first openly LGBT premier of a Canadian province and Ontario's first woman in the office.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Xavier Bettel takes office as Luxembourg's prime minister
description: Xavier Bettel took office on 4 December 2013 as Luxembourg's first openly gay prime minister.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Etienne Schneider takes office as Luxembourg's deputy prime minister
description: Etienne Schneider assumed office on 4 December 2013 as Luxembourg's first openly gay deputy prime minister.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Luxembourg seats openly gay prime minister and deputy together
description: With Xavier Bettel and Etienne Schneider both in office from December 2013, Luxembourg became the first country whose prime minister and deputy prime minister were both openly gay.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly transgender and bisexual UK parliamentarians
description: In 2013 Nikki Sinclaire came out as transgender, the first sitting UK parliamentarian to do so, while Daniel Kawczynski became the first British MP to come out as bisexual.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Benjamin Medrano elected as Mexico's first openly gay mayor
description: Benjamin Medrano won the mayoralty of Fresnillo in 2013, the first openly gay person elected mayor in Mexico.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First UN ministerial meeting on LGBT rights
description: The United Nations hosted its first ministerial-level meeting on the rights of lesbian, gay, bisexual and transgender people in 2013. Representatives of ten countries and the EU, joined by leading human-rights organizations, committed to jointly counter discrimination and violence against LGBT people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Obama raises gay rights in a US inaugural address
description: In his 2013 inaugural address, Barack Obama spoke of gay people and their rights, the first mention of the topic in a US presidential inauguration speech.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: White House hosts its first bisexual-specific event
description: On Celebrate Bisexuality Day 2013 the White House held a closed-door meeting with around 30 bisexual advocates and government officials, the first event any White House had dedicated specifically to bisexual issues.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex spouse of a US federal lawmaker receives a House Spouse ID
description: Philip Frank, husband of Representative Mark Pocan, officially received a House Spouse ID in 2013, the first same-sex spouse of a US federal lawmaker to be issued one deliberately.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nitza Quiñones Alejandro confirmed as US federal judge
description: The US Senate confirmed Nitza Quiñones Alejandro to a federal judgeship in 2013, making her the first openly gay Latina to serve as a federal judge.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Eric Fanning becomes acting secretary of the US Air Force
description: Eric Fanning, who is openly gay, took over as acting secretary of the US Air Force in 2013, at that point the highest-ranking openly LGBT official at the Department of Defense.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Todd Hughes becomes first openly gay US circuit judge
description: Todd Hughes was confirmed to a US federal appeals court in 2013, the first openly gay judge at the circuit-court level.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Robbie Rogers comes out and joins the LA Galaxy
description: Footballer Robbie Rogers came out as gay on 15 February 2013, the only fully capped male international in the sport to have done so at the time, and later joined the Los Angeles Galaxy as the first openly gay man to play in Major League Soccer.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jason Collins comes out as gay
description: NBA player Jason Collins came out on 29 April 2013, the first active male athlete in a major North American professional team sport to publicly say he is gay.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Fallon Fox comes out as transgender
description: Mixed martial artist Fallon Fox came out in 2013, becoming the first openly transgender athlete in the sport's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jallen Messersmith comes out in US college basketball
description: Jallen Messersmith of Benedictine College in Atchison, Kansas, came out in 2013 and is reported as the first openly gay player in US men's college basketball.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cason Crane completes the Seven Summits
description: In 2013 climber Cason Crane became the first openly gay man to summit the Seven Summits and the first person to carry the rainbow flag to the top of Mount Everest.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First UFC bout between two openly gay fighters
description: Liz Carmouche and Jessica Andrade met in 2013 in the first UFC match contested by two openly gay fighters.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Darren Young comes out as gay
description: Professional wrestler Darren Young (Fred Rosser) came out in 2013, the first active professional wrestler to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Philadelphia passes broad transgender rights bill
description: In 2013 Philadelphia enacted one of the most comprehensive city-level transgender rights measures in the United States, covering bathroom access and making it the first east-coast city to fund transition-related healthcare for municipal employees.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Defense Department records first gender change for a retiree
description: Transgender veteran Autumn Sandeen had her gender updated to female in the Pentagon's enrollment system effective 12 April 2013, described by advocates as the first such recognition by the Department of Defense for anyone in a uniformed capacity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ben Barres joins the US National Academy of Sciences
description: Neuroscientist Ben Barres was elected to the US National Academy of Sciences in 2013, its first openly transgender member.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: California recommends a transgender-themed book for schools
description: In 2013 the California Department of Education's recommended reading list for grades pre-K through 12 included a book with a transgender theme for the first time, I Am J by Cris Beam.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: California enacts first US law protecting transgender students
description: California's School Success and Opportunity Act, enacted in 2013, was the first US law protecting transgender students, guaranteeing public-school pupils participation in sex-segregated programs and access to facilities matching their gender identity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jennifer Pritzker comes out as transgender
description: Jennifer Pritzker came out in 2013, becoming the world's first openly transgender billionaire.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Argentina updates identity documents for a transgender child
description: In 2013 a six-year-old girl named Luana became the first transgender child in Argentina to have her name officially changed on her identity documents, believed to be the youngest person to use the country's 2012 Gender Identity Law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jennifer Finney Boylan named co-chair of GLAAD's board
description: Author Jennifer Finney Boylan was chosen in 2013 as the first openly transgender co-chair of GLAAD's National Board of Directors.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Paris Lees appears on BBC Question Time
description: On 31 October 2013 Paris Lees became the first openly transgender panellist on the BBC's Question Time, drawing praise from senior British politicians.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stephen Alexander comes out as transgender
description: Stephen Alexander of Rhode Island came out publicly in 2013, the first US high-school coach known to do so as transgender.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Audrey Gauthier elected union local president in Canada
description: On 1 November 2013 Audrey Gauthier was elected president of CUPE 4041, which represents Air Transat flight attendants in Montreal, becoming the first openly transgender person to head a union local in Canada.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Australian Senate publishes first parliamentary report on intersex people
description: On 25 October 2013 the Australian Senate published the first parliamentary report anywhere to examine the human rights and health of intersex people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kristin Beck comes out as transgender
description: Kristin Beck came out in 2013 as the first openly transgender retired US Navy SEAL.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US allows first same-sex spouse burial in a national cemetery
description: In 2013 the US Department of Veterans Affairs permitted, for the first time, the burial of a veteran's same-sex spouse in a national cemetery: the ashes of Nancy Lynchild, spouse of retired Air Force officer Linda Campbell, were interred at Willamette National Cemetery in Oregon.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Muslim lesbian couple enters a UK civil partnership
description: Rehana Kausar and Sobia Kamar, both from Pakistan, entered a civil partnership in the United Kingdom in 2013, reported as the first Muslim lesbian couple to do so there.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
